name: pm.sample
description: Draw samples from the posterior using MCMC
gotchas: nutpie doesn't store log_likelihood automatically — call pm.compute_log_likelihood after
Use nuts_sampler='nutpie' for 2-5x speedup
Always set random_seed for reproducibility
Set target_accept=0.95+ for difficult geometries (funnels, horseshoe)

name: pm.sample_prior_predictive
description: Sample from the prior predictive distribution to check priors
gotchas: Always check prior predictive before sampling — catches unreasonable priors early
Returns InferenceData with prior and prior_predictive groups

name: pm.sample_posterior_predictive
description: Generate posterior predictive samples for model checking
gotchas: Use extend_inferencedata=True to add posterior_predictive group to existing idata
Requires observed data to be in the model

name: pm.compute_log_likelihood
description: Compute log likelihood for model comparison (LOO-CV, WAIC)
gotchas: Required before calling az.loo or az.compare
nutpie sampler doesn't compute this automatically — must call explicitly
Can be memory-intensive for large datasets

name: pm.find_constrained_prior
description: Find distribution parameters so that mass% of prior is between lower and upper
gotchas: Useful for eliciting informative priors from domain knowledge
Works with most continuous distributions

name: pm.Model
description: Context manager for defining a Bayesian model
gotchas: Always use coords and dims for labeled dimensions
Define coords dict before the model context
Use pm.Data for mutable data that can be updated for predictions

name: pm.Data
description: Create a shared variable that can be updated for out-of-sample predictions
gotchas: Use pm.set_data() to update values after sampling
Always specify dims for proper labeling
Useful for counterfactual predictions and cross-validation

name: pm.Deterministic
description: Create a deterministic variable tracked in the trace
gotchas: Use for derived quantities you want to analyze (e.g., transformed parameters)
Does not add to the model's log probability
Must specify dims if the variable has named dimensions

name: pm.Potential
description: Add an arbitrary factor to the model log probability
gotchas: Use for constraints, soft bounds, or custom likelihoods
Value is added directly to log_p — must be a log probability
Does not create a random variable in the trace

name: pm.do
description: Perform a causal do-intervention (set variables to fixed values)
gotchas: Part of PyMC's causal inference API
interventions is a dict mapping variable names to values
Returns a new model with the intervention applied

name: pm.observe
description: Condition the model on observed data (for causal inference)
gotchas: Used with pm.do for causal queries
observations is a dict mapping variable names to observed values

name: pm.model_to_graphviz
description: Create a graphviz visualization of the model structure
gotchas: Requires graphviz system package installed
Useful for model documentation and debugging

name: pm.Normal
description: Normal (Gaussian) distribution
gotchas: sigma is standard deviation, not variance
Use for continuous unbounded parameters

name: pm.HalfNormal
description: Half-normal distribution (positive values only)
gotchas: Good default prior for scale parameters
sigma controls the spread; the mode is always at 0

name: pm.StudentT
description: Student's t-distribution — heavier tails than Normal
gotchas: nu (degrees of freedom) controls tail heaviness; nu=1 is Cauchy, nu>30 is ~Normal
Use for robust regression or heavy-tailed likelihoods (financial data)

name: pm.Bernoulli
description: Bernoulli distribution for binary outcomes
gotchas: Use logit_p for numerical stability in logistic regression
Exactly one of p or logit_p must be specified

name: pm.Poisson
description: Poisson distribution for count data
gotchas: mu must be positive — use pm.math.exp(linear_predictor)
Assumes mean equals variance; use NegativeBinomial for overdispersion

name: pm.NormalMixture
signature: pm.NormalMixture(name, w, mu, sigma=None, tau=None, dims=None, ...)
description: Mixture of Normal distributions
gotchas: w = mixture weights (must sum to 1, use Dirichlet prior)
Use ordered transform on mu to prevent label switching
sigma can be scalar (shared) or vector (per-component)

name: pm.Censored
description: Censored distribution wrapper for survival/censored data
gotchas: dist should be an unnamed distribution: pm.Normal.dist(mu, sigma)
Use lower for left-censoring, upper for right-censoring, both for interval
The .dist() method creates a distribution without adding it to the model

name: pm.OrderedLogistic
description: Ordered logistic regression likelihood for ordinal outcomes
gotchas: cutpoints must be ordered — use transform=pm.distributions.transforms.ordered
eta is the linear predictor (scalar per observation)
Number of cutpoints = number of categories - 1
Don't name cutpoints variable 'cutpoints' if using 'cutpoints' as dim name

name: pm.ZeroInflatedPoisson
description: Zero-inflated Poisson for count data with excess zeros
gotchas: psi is the probability of the Poisson component (not the zero component)
Use when data has more zeros than a Poisson model predicts

name: pm.Dirichlet
description: Dirichlet distribution over simplices (probabilities that sum to 1)
gotchas: a = concentration parameters; all 1s gives uniform over simplex
Use as prior for mixture weights or categorical probabilities
Output sums to 1 by construction

name: pm.Beta
description: Beta distribution on [0, 1]
gotchas: alpha=beta=1 is Uniform(0,1)
Use for probability parameters or proportions

name: pm.Exponential
description: Exponential distribution (positive values, memoryless)
gotchas: lam is the rate (1/mean), not the scale
Good prior for scale parameters when you expect them to be small

name: pm.InverseGamma
description: Inverse gamma distribution (positive values)
gotchas: Conjugate prior for Normal variance (not standard deviation)
Can have very heavy tails — consider HalfNormal or HalfCauchy instead

name: pm.HalfCauchy
description: Half-Cauchy distribution (positive values, heavy-tailed)
gotchas: Very heavy tails — good weakly informative prior for scale parameters
beta is the scale parameter (mode is at 0)

name: pm.Uniform
description: Uniform distribution
gotchas: Rarely a good prior choice — can cause sampling problems at boundaries
Use informative priors (Normal, HalfNormal) instead when possible

name: pm.gp.HSGP
description: Hilbert Space Gaussian Process approximation — fast GP for large datasets
gotchas: m = number of basis functions (start with 20-30, increase if fit is poor)
c = proportion of data range for boundary extension (default varies)
Much faster than full GP — O(nm) vs O(n^3)
Use prior_linearized() for the most efficient parameterization

name: pm.gp.HSGPPeriodic
description: HSGP approximation for periodic Gaussian processes
gotchas: m = number of basis functions for the periodic component
cov_func must be a periodic covariance function
Good for seasonal patterns in time series

name: pm.gp.Marginal
description: Marginal Gaussian process (integrates out the GP values analytically)
gotchas: Exact GP — O(n^3) cost, use HSGP for n > 500
Use .marginal_likelihood() for the observed data likelihood
Use .conditional() for predictions at new points

name: pm.AR
signature: pm.AR(name, rho, sigma=None, init_dist=None, steps=None, dims=None, ...)
description: Autoregressive process of order p
gotchas: rho can be scalar (AR(1)) or vector (AR(p))
init_dist specifies the distribution for initial values
Use for time series with autoregressive structure

name: pm.GaussianRandomWalk
description: Gaussian random walk process
gotchas: sigma controls the step-to-step variance (innovation)
Use for latent processes that evolve smoothly (stochastic volatility, trends)
init_dist sets the distribution for the first value
